query:
Research the company "penskeautomotive".

Research [Company Name]: (1) Recent earnings calls or guidance changes (especially missed guidance or reduced forward guidance in past 12-18 months), (2) P&L performance gaps or margin pressure (identify specific quarterly misses or margin shortfalls), (3) Production/manufacturing capacity and growth rates, (4) Distribution channels and expansion activities, (5) Pricing strategy changes or pricing power challenges, (6) Marketing/media spend efficiency metrics (ROAS, CAC trends), (7) Organizational changes (new CEO, CFO, or Chief Strategy Officer appointments in past 12-24 months), (8) Strategic review announcements or business model pivot signals, (9) Market segment focus (especially midsize platform launches or product line expansions), (10) Cross-functional challenges between product, supply chain, pricing, and go-to-market functions.

Focus on the last 6 months. Return a comprehensive summary with specific facts, numbers, and dates.
answer:
# Penske Automotive Group (PAG) Research Report
**Date:** June 12, 2026

## Executive Summary
Penske Automotive Group (PAG) is currently navigating a transitional period marked by Q1 2026 margin compression, declining commercial truck volumes, and severe headwinds in Battery Electric Vehicle (BEV) sales. To counter these pressures, the company has executed a strategic pivot toward "high-volume premium" consolidation, acquiring roughly $2 billion in annualized Toyota and Lexus revenues over the past six months. While digital marketing efficiencies and strong service revenues are providing downside protection, the company faces tightening pricing power, constrained used vehicle supply, and an unresolved discrepancy regarding its Chief Financial Officer appointment.

---

## Detailed Analysis

### 1. Financial Performance & Margin Pressures
Penske has experienced notable P&L gaps and margin compression over the last 6-12 months, culminating in a challenging first quarter of 2026. 
*   **Revenue and Earnings Declines:** In Q1 2026, revenue decreased to $7.9 billion from $8.0 billion in Q1 2025 [Earnings slip as Penske Automotive (NYSE: PAG) posts Q1 2026 EPS of $3.56](https://www.stocktitan.net/sec-filings/PAG/8-k-penske-automotive-group-inc-reports-material-event-2698d29f0c2f.html). Net income fell to $234.5 million (down from $257.7 million in Q1 2025), and adjusted EPS dropped 15% year-over-year to $3.05 [PENSKE AUTOMOTIVE GROUP REPORTS QUARTERLY RESULTS](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/penske-automotive-group-reports-quarterly-results-302756488.html). 
*   **Missed Targets:** The company previously missed revenue forecasts in Q2 2025 [Earnings call transcript: Penske Automotive Q2 2025 beats EPS forecasts](https://www.investing.com/news/transcripts/earnings-call-transcript-penske-automotive-q2-2025-beats-eps-forecasts-93CH-4160973) and saw premium luxury sales miss targets in Q4 2025 [Penske Automotive saw premium luxury sales miss targets in Q4 2025 | WardsAuto](https://www.wardsauto.com/news/penske-automotive-saw-premium-luxury-sales-miss-targets-in-q4-2025/812265/).
*   **Unit Margin Compression:** Gross profit per new retail unit fell 4.6% to $4,783 in Q1 2026, while gross profit per used retail unit declined 2.0% to $2,076 [PENSKE AUTOMOTIVE GROUP REPORTS QUARTERLY RESULTS](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/penske-automotive-group-reports-quarterly-results-302756488.html).
*   **SG&A Increases:** Selling, General, and Administrative (SG&A) expenses as a percentage of gross profit rose to 74.3% in Q1 2026, up from 72.0% a year prior [PENSKE AUTOMOTIVE GROUP REPORTS QUARTERLY RESULTS](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/penske-automotive-group-reports-quarterly-results-302756488.html). This was exacerbated by $10.5 million in added UK social program costs, rent, and real estate taxes, alongside a $6 million negative impact from Jan/Feb 2026 weather disruptions [Penske Automotive Group, Inc. (NYSE:PAG) Q1 2026 Earnings Call Transcript - Insider Monkey](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/penske-automotive-group-inc-nysepag-q1-2026-earnings-call-transcript-1751000/).
*   **Commercial Truck Weakness:** Retail commercial truck sales plummeted 24% in Q1 2026 to 3,583 units, driving segment earnings before taxes (EBT) down to $36.4 million from $45.1 million [PENSKE AUTOMOTIVE GROUP REPORTS QUARTERLY RESULTS](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/penske-automotive-group-reports-quarterly-results-302756488.html). Additionally, Penske Transportation Solutions (PTS) saw its gain on the sale of used trucks decline by $26 million YoY in Q1 2026 [Penske Automotive Group, Inc. (NYSE:PAG) Q1 2026 Earnings Call Transcript - Insider Monkey](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/penske-automotive-group-inc-nysepag-q1-2026-earnings-call-transcript-1751000/).

### 2. Strategic Pivot & Distribution Expansion
Penske has officially signaled a strategic shift away from volume-focused retailing toward a luxury-centric portfolio, specifically targeting "high-volume premium" consolidation [The Lexus Standard: Inside Penske Automotive Group’s Pivot to Premium Dominance | FinancialContent](https://www.financialcontent.com/article/finterra-2026-1-26-the-lexus-standard-inside-penske-automotive-groups-pivot-to-premium-dominance); [What is Sales and Marketing Strategy of Penske Automotive Group Company? – MatrixBCG.com](https://matrixbcg.com/blogs/marketing-strategy/penskeautomotive).
*   **Premium Acquisitions:** Between November 2025 and February 2026, Penske acquired two Toyota and four Lexus dealerships, adding an estimated $2 billion in annualized revenue [PENSKE AUTOMOTIVE GROUP REPORTS QUARTERLY RESULTS](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/penske-automotive-group-reports-quarterly-results-302756488.html). This included the November 2025 acquisition of Penske Motor Group ($1.5 billion annualized revenue), which features Longo Toyota, the largest Toyota dealership in the U.S. [Penske Automotive Group Expands With Acquisition Of Four High-Volume Toyota And Lexus Dealerships](https://pulse2.com/penske-automotive-group/). In February 2026, the company added Lexus of Orlando and Lexus of Winter Park ($450 million annualized revenue) [Penske Automotive Group, Inc. - PENSKE AUTOMOTIVE GROUP EXPANDS PRESENCE IN FLORIDA](https://investors.penskeautomotive.com/news/news-details/2026/PENSKE-AUTOMOTIVE-GROUP-EXPANDS-PRESENCE-IN-FLORIDA/default.aspx).
*   **Divestitures:** To fund this pivot, Penske divested 23 non-strategic retail dealerships over the two years preceding February 2026, shedding approximately $700 million in revenue [PENSKE AUTOMOTIVE GROUP REPORTS FOURTH QUARTER AND FULL YEAR 2025 RESULTS](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/penske-automotive-group-reports-fourth-quarter-and-full-year-2025-results-302684889.html).
*   **Service Capacity Growth:** Premier Truck Group (PTG) set a target to increase North American service bay capacity by 10% by the end of 2025 to capture higher-margin maintenance demand [What is Growth Strategy and Future Prospects of Penske Automotive Group Company? – MatrixBCG.com](https://matrixbcg.com/blogs/growth-strategy/penskeautomotive). This strategy is already yielding results, with U.S. same-store service and parts revenues rising 6% in late 2025 [How to Approach Penske Automotive Stock Post Q4 Earnings? | Nasdaq](https://www.nasdaq.com/articles/how-approach-penske-automotive-stock-post-q4-earnings).
*   **Used Vehicle Footprint:** Penske has slowed its standalone CarShop expansion, transitioning U.K. CarShop locations to Sytner Select dealerships in late 2024/early 2025 [Earnings call: Penske Automotive Group reports steady growth in Q3 2024 By Investing.com](https://uk.investing.com/news/stock-market-news/earnings-call-penske-automotive-group-reports-steady-growth-in-q3-2024-93CH-3762886). The company aims for an annual throughput of over 300,000 used units by 2026 [What is Growth Strategy and Future Prospects of Penske Automotive Group Company? – MatrixBCG.com](https://matrixbcg.com/blogs/growth-strategy/penskeautomotive).

### 3. Pricing Power & Market Dynamics
Penske is facing distinct pricing power challenges across multiple segments:
*   **Loss of MSRP Pricing Power:** In Q4 2025, only 25% of new units were sold at MSRP, down from 29% in Q4 2024 [Earnings call transcript: Penske Automotive Q4 2025 misses EPS forecast, revenue rises By Investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/transcripts/earnings-call-transcript-penske-automotive-q4-2025-misses-eps-forecast-revenue-rises-93CH-4501072).
*   **BEV Inventory Bloat:** Due to the elimination of tax credits and easing emissions regulations, Q1 2026 BEV sales crashed 61% YoY. Consequently, BEV inventory supply swelled to 78 days, far exceeding the overall new car average of 43 days [Penske Automotive Group, Inc. (NYSE:PAG) Q1 2026 Earnings Call Transcript - Insider Monkey](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/penske-automotive-group-inc-nysepag-q1-2026-earnings-call-transcript-1751000/).
*   **Used Vehicle Supply Constraints:** Used sales are constrained by consumer affordability and a lack of lease returns. Lease returns fell to ~7% of used sales in late 2025 (down from 11% in 2024), though management expects this to improve in the second half of 2026 [Penske Automotive Balances Headwinds With Strong Cash Flow - The Globe and Mail](https://www.theglobeandmail.com/investing/markets/stocks/PAG-N/pressreleases/218074/penske-automotive-balances-headwinds-with-strong-cash-flow/); [Penske Automotive saw premium luxury sales miss targets in Q4 2025 | WardsAuto](https://www.wardsauto.com/news/penske-automotive-saw-premium-luxury-sales-miss-targets-in-q4-2025/812265/).
*   **International Headwinds:** The UK market is pressured by inflation and higher taxes, while the European market faces increasing competition from Chinese OEMs [Earnings call transcript: Penske Automotive Q4 2025 misses EPS forecast, revenue rises By Investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/transcripts/earnings-call-transcript-penske-automotive-q4-2025-misses-eps-forecast-revenue-rises-93CH-4501072); [Penske Automotive Q4 2025 slides: Revenue dips 4% amid mixed segment performance By Investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/company-news/penske-automotive-q4-2025-slides-revenue-dips-4-amid-mixed-segment-performance-93CH-4501090).

### 4. Marketing & Media Spend Efficiency
Penske has heavily digitized its go-to-market motion to drive sales efficiency:
*   **Digital Allocation:** Over 70% of total marketing spend in 2025 was directed to digital channels, which generated over 20% of total retail leads [What is Sales and Marketing Strategy of Penske Automotive Group Company? – MatrixBCG.com](https://matrixbcg.com/blogs/marketing-strategy/penskeautomotive); [What is Growth Strategy and Future Prospects of Penske Automotive Group Company? – MatrixBCG.com](https://matrixbcg.com/blogs/growth-strategy/penskeautomotive).
*   **Targeting & Conversion:** The company utilizes the Salesforce Automotive Cloud CRM for segmentation, achieving a 15% higher email conversion rate than the industry average. In 2025, the budget was shifted toward social video and luxury-influencer partnerships to target younger premium buyers [What is Sales and Marketing Strategy of Penske Automotive Group Company? – MatrixBCG.com](https://matrixbcg.com/blogs/marketing-strategy/penskeautomotive).
*   **Digital Retailing:** Penske's proprietary platform now allows customers to complete nearly 95% of the vehicle purchase process online [What is Growth Strategy and Future Prospects of Penske Automotive Group Company? – MatrixBCG.com](https://matrixbcg.com/blogs/growth-strategy/penskeautomotive).

### 5. Organizational Changes
There have been several notable leadership shifts, alongside a significant reporting discrepancy regarding the CFO role:
*   **Executive Leadership:** Roger S. Penske remains Chairman and CEO (2025 compensation: $8.77 million), Robert H. Kurnick, Jr. remains President, and Greg Penske has been elevated to Vice Chair [PENSKE AUTOMOTIVE GROUP, INC. ($PAG) CEO 2025 Pay Revealed | Quiver Quantitative](https://www.quiverquant.com/news/PENSKE+AUTOMOTIVE+GROUP,+INC.+($PAG)+CEO+2025+Pay+Revealed); [FinancialContent - The Lexus Standard: Inside Penske Automotive Group’s Pivot to Premium Dominance](https://markets.financialcontent.com/stocks/article/finterra-2026-1-26-the-lexus-standard-inside-penske-automotive-groups-pivot-to-premium-dominance).
*   **CFO Discrepancy:** J.D. Carlson was reportedly named EVP and CFO effective February 23, 2026 [Penske Appoints New CFO | F&I and Showroom](https://www.fi-magazine.com/news/penske-appoints-new-cfo). However, SEC filings as recently as March 20, 2026, still identified Michelle (Shelley) Hulgrave as EVP and CFO, and she reportedly sold shares in that capacity on March 21, 2026 [PENSKE AUTOMOTIVE GROUP, INC. ($PAG) CEO 2025 Pay Revealed | Quiver Quantitative](https://www.quiverquant.com/news/PENSKE+AUTOMOTIVE+GROUP,+INC.+($PAG)+CEO+2025+Pay+Revealed). 
*   **Regional Leadership:** In April 2026, Stephen was appointed as the new General Manager for Penske New Zealand [Penske Australia & New Zealand - About Us](https://www.penskeanz.com/about-us/).

---

## Gaps and Limitations
Based on the provided research findings, the following requested information could not be verified:
*   **Specific Marketing Metrics:** Exact figures for Return on Ad Spend (ROAS) and Customer Acquisition Cost (CAC) trends were not available in the source data.
*   **Chief Strategy Officer:** There is no mention of a Chief Strategy Officer appointment in the past 12-24 months.
*   **Cross-Functional Challenges:** No explicit data or internal reports were provided regarding cross-functional friction between product, supply chain, pricing, and go-to-market teams.
*   **CFO Clarity:** The conflicting reports regarding whether J.D. Carlson or Michelle Hulgrave currently holds the CFO position cannot be definitively resolved using the provided text.
